title: roadmap roadmap.yaml
description: Canonical roadmap graph: PIs containing sprints, with dependency edges, file-ownership for two-wave detection, session estimates, gates, and per-sprint kickoff briefs. SLICES.md is generated from this file; do not edit the generated markdown.

north_star.description: Path to the strategy / vision source of truth

default_gate.description: Verification gate inherited by sprints whose gate is 'default' or interpolates {{default}}

worktree_root.description: Parent dir for per-sprint worktrees; use a WSL/Linux-fs path, NOT a OneDrive-synced checkout

worktree_gb.description: Calibration knob for the disk ceiling: GB one worktree really costs (checkout + installs + build litter). Omitted → estimated from the tracked tree's size × 1.3

worker_mode.description: Default --permission-mode for launched worker + lead sessions; --worker-mode overrides. 'auto' = auto-approve tool/bash/MCP with safety checks (NOT a bypass); 'plan' = read-only research that gates edits behind plan approval.

agent_cmd.description: Launch-command template for INTERACTIVE worker/lead sessions — swap the agent (e.g. a codex invocation). Tokens: {mode} = permission mode, {prompt} = the quoted kickoff prompt. Default: 'claude --permission-mode {mode} {prompt}'. Autonomous headless launches stay claude.

completed_window_days.description: How recently a sprint must have completed to appear in the Recently-completed table

base_branch.description: Branch worktrees are cut from + PR base

remote.description: Git remote name for fetch + worktree base ref

default_weight.description: Weight assumed for a slice whose gate runner isn't recognized and that touches no code (default light)

weight_patterns.description: Extend the built-in multi-ecosystem runner classifier with repo-specific regexes (case-insensitive strings)

weight_cost.description: Override per-class per-session resource cost used by the concurrency recommender

initiatives.description: Optional per-initiative Linear styling: maps an initiative NAME (matching a pi.initiative) to its board icon/color, so grouping reads as signal (every Lumen project a WritingAI icon, every Trust project a Shield) instead of the deterministic-but-arbitrary fallback palette. Applied to a declared initiative's projects AND its header; undeclared initiatives keep Linear's default header and use the fallback palette on their projects only.

icon.description: Linear icon name (e.g. Robot, Shield, Database, WritingAI). Names are a fixed Linear set; an unrecognized one degrades to no icon (color still groups).

plate.description: The 'My Issues' hopper: an explicit list of slice invoke keys / backlog ids the Linear sync assigns to YOU (assignee), so Linear's My Issues tab = your curated current batch. Presence enables the feature (absent → no assignee projection at all). Active slices / in_progress items are ALWAYS included on top; completed slices auto-drain on sync. Capped for signal by meta.linear.plate_max. Issues we assign carry a 'plate' label so a hand-assignment in Linear is never disturbed.

linear.description: Optional Linear integration. Absent → all Linear behavior off (byte-identical). Linear is a PROJECTION of this file plus a proposal inbox — the YAML stays canonical. Auth is the LINEAR_API_KEY env var, never stored here.

granularity.description: What projects to Linear: PIs as Projects only, +slices as Issues, +backlog items too. Per-PI override via pi.linear.granularity (ack-gated on conflict).

verbosity.description: Issue-description detail. Never copies read-order/prompt; always ends with the machine footer + docs link.

pull.description: Inbound handling: propose = /sync walks the inbox with you; auto = applied directly.

push_on.description: sync = /sync runs the Linear phase when authed; manual = only 'roadmap linear sync'.

estimate_max.description: Max value pushed to a slice's native Linear estimate (est_sessions is rounded, then clamped here). Set to your Linear team's estimate scale max — use the 'Linear' scale (1..5; extended adds 6,7) for a direct 1-point=1-session read. validate warns when a slice exceeds it: the signal to split it, not extend.

plate_max.description: Cap on the EXPLICIT meta.plate list — validate warns above it so Linear's My Issues stays signal (active work adds on top, uncapped).

status_map.description: roadmap status → exact Linear workflow-state NAME overrides; unmapped statuses use the state-TYPE defaults (scheduled→backlog, next→unstarted, active/blocked/paused/gated→started, complete→completed).

watch.description: Inbound sources (multiple teams/projects, e.g. a public 'Submit an issue' team). New issues become proposed backlog items carrying source.linear demarcation.

project.description: Omit to watch the whole team

priority.description: Default priority for inbound items = implicit source weighting (the issue's own Linear priority wins when set)

discipline.description: Scope-discipline knobs read by the /sync and /debrief guardrails and the wave scheduler

capture_ratio.description: Max (captured backlog items + added sprints) per completed slice per review window before the sprawl warning fires

coherence.description: Wave-packing coherence: prefer finishing started PIs over opening fresh ones (strictly below declared priority). false restores pure priority/status/est ordering.

last_review.description: The /debrief / /retro anchor: when the human last reviewed, and the roadmap.yaml git rev AT that review (captured before the review's own edits). Written by the skills via Edit+validate.

commit.description: git rev of the previous review's roadmap.yaml state

links.description: Optional repo cross-references the renderer links from (all optional; omitted ones are skipped)

cost.description: Per-session resource cost for a weight class

status.description: Canonical status. Emoji derived: active 🟢 · next 🟡 · scheduled ⚪ · complete ✅ · blocked 🔴 · paused ⏸️ · gated 🔒 · optionality 🟣

id.description: Stable PI slug; matches the branch-slug and the tracker row

program_label.description: Display string for the 'PI / program' column when it differs from id (e.g. 'Billing Rewrite P2'). Optional; defaults to the uppercased id.

detail.description: Pointer to the sprint dir / detail doc

exec_hint.description: Optional human override of the derived exec-plan line; rendered alongside the derived line

initiative.description: Groups this PI under a Linear Initiative of this name (the tier above projects). Sync creates the initiative and attaches this PI's project. Several PIs sharing a name group together.

priority.description: STRATEGIC priority of this bet (not derived from slice heat) → Linear project priority. tier P0=Urgent … P3=Low.

summary.description: Short one-line subtitle → Linear project description (the ≤255 field the board shows under the title). Authored so it never truncates; when omitted the subtitle is derived from the exit's first sentence and validate warns if that would truncate.

start_date.description: Start date (YYYY-MM-DD) → Linear project startDate (drives the roadmap timeline). Set it explicitly, or leave blank and the sync auto-stamps it the first time the PI is active (≈ when it was picked up). Explicit always wins.

target_date.description: Target date (YYYY-MM-DD) → Linear project targetDate. A deliberate commitment date; omit when there's no dated commitment.

linear.description: Per-PI Linear override + the pushed-back project id. A granularity conflicting with the global requires the yes_linear_override ack at creation; validate warns on stored mismatches.

project.description: Linear project id — written back by the first push; don't hand-author

id.description: Sprint slug local to its PI, e.g. s1, s3, m1-s2

status_label.description: Display override for the status cell (e.g. 'Planned', 'Queued', 'Gated (Connor)', 'Next'); defaults to the capitalized enum

invoke.description: The /slice invocation key. Stable — renames break references. Unique across the whole file.

what.description: One-line summary for the At-a-glance table

est_sessions.description: Focused Claude sessions remaining to clear this sprint (0 when done)

weight.description: Optional override of the inferred resource weight (heavy=dotnet test, medium=build/vitest, light=docs); drives the concurrency recommender

deps.description: Dependency edges: sibling sprint id (s2), fully-qualified pi-id/sprint-id, or a PI id (complete-when-all-sprints-complete)

touches.description: Files this sprint writes; used to detect shared-file contention (two-wave) in the scheduler

gate.description: 'default' (inherit meta.default_gate) or a string; {{default}} interpolates meta.default_gate

gated_on.description: Name of the human this is gated on; such a node never enters the auto-ready set

read_order.description: Ordered docs/sections a session reads to self-orient (markdown link or 'path — note')

resume_action.description: The next action; first sentence is the immediate step

kickoff_brief.description: 'brief' = synthesize from fields; or an inline brief string; or a path to a brief file

completed_on.description: ISO date a sprint flipped to complete (drives the Recently-completed window)

track.description: Optional lane label for the three-track partition (e.g. A | B | C). --track filters a fanout to one lane. Omit for an untracked slice.

prompt.description: Author-stashed pickup instructions, embedded VERBATIM in the synthesized kickoff brief and shown by /slice and roadmap show. Update as new info comes in (set_fields / roadmap set).

linear.description: Linear issue identifier (e.g. ABC-123) this slice maps to — written back by the first push. The {linear} branch_convention token uses it so Linear auto-links the PR.

priority.description: Optional prioritization: tier for coarse triage buckets, weight for fine ordering within a tier, reason so agents and humans know why. Sort order is derived (tier asc, weight desc), never stored. Shared by roadmap sprints and backlog items.

execution.description: Per-slice staffing-strategy HINT: how to fan this slice out (topology + suggested LIVE worker count + team composition). All fields optional and backward-compatible — a slice that omits the block behaves exactly as before; the lead chooses by gut. Rendered as an imperative directive so a launched session staffs at the declared topology instead of chronically under-parallelizing.

mode.description: Fan-out topology. solo = atomic/exploratory/branching-sequential (no fan-out). subagents = scoped sprint, lead-merges + disjoint-file workers (the current default). dynamic-workflow = an in-slice pipeline whose steps depend on each other. agent-team = many genuinely-independent file-clusters + peer coordination (native Agent Teams).

min_concurrency.description: Floor — warn/refuse if a run uses fewer on disjoint files. Must be ≤ concurrency when both are set.

team.description: Team composition; omit for solo. When concurrency is also set, the head-count must agree.

rationale.description: Why this topology + count (surfaced verbatim in the directive)
